### Smart Comfort AI-Enabled CPAP Personalization
...
First FDA-cleared AI-enabled medical device that recommends personalized comfort settings for CPAP therapy
...
Uses machine learning algorithms trained on 100+ million nights of sleep data to adjust pressure ramp, exhale pressure relief, and therapy initiation settings. Launches in early 2026 as limited beta for myAir users with AirSense 11 devices.
...
### Dawn AI Sleep Health Concierge
...
Generative AI-enabled digital assistant that provides 24/7 sleep health guidance and CPAP therapy support
...
Offers personalized guidance using ResMed's sleep health knowledge repository and real-world evidence. Provides lifestyle coaching and therapy support but does not make medical recommendations.
...
### AWS SageMaker Sleep Therapy Personalization
...
Cloud-based machine learning platform for personalizing sleep therapy for millions of patients
...
Partnership with AWS to leverage cloud computing and machine learning for analyzing patient data and optimizing therapy outcomes at scale.
...
Smart Comfort is the first FDA-cleared AI-enabled medical device that recommends personalized comfort settings for CPAP therapy, using machine learning trained on over 100 million nights of sleep data to optimize pressure ramp, exhale pressure relief, and therapy initiation settings.
...
ResMed uses AI through multiple tools including Smart Comfort for personalized settings, Dawn AI concierge for 24/7 guidance, and Compliance Coach to identify early signs of therapy abandonment and provide interventions to keep patients engaged.
...
ResMed has the world's largest repository of sleep health data with 20+ billion nights of data from 28 million patients, 100% cloud-connected devices, and proprietary machine learning algorithms that provide personalized therapy recommendations at scale.
...
| Application | Vendor | Use Case |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Smart Comfort | ResMed (proprietary) | AI-enabled personalization of CPAP comfort settings using machine learning on 100+ million nights of sleep data |
| Dawn AI Concierge | ResMed (proprietary) | Generative AI-powered sleep health assistant providing 24/7 guidance and therapy support |
| Amazon SageMaker | AWS | Cloud-based machine learning platform for analyzing patient data and personalizing sleep therapy |
| Compliance Coach | ResMed (proprietary) | AI system that helps improve CPAP therapy adherence by identifying and preventing therapy abandonment |

results.title: Nadi Sadr
highlights:
Advanced Data Scientist | ResMed
...
Advanced Data Scientist at [Resmed](https://www.linkedin.com/company/resmed)
...
I currently work as a data scientist at ResMed, one of the leading medical device companies in Australia and worldwide, after eight years of experience in academia on data science and machine learning, developing technical engineering solutions and AI algorithms for different applications. I previously worked at Clifford Lab, United States, as a data scientist, designing and organizing the International Data Science George Moody PhysioNet Challenges. My role was to prepare and curate data and cover every aspect of a data science competition from designing machine learning algorithms and predictive models, managing the datasets and processing the algorithms on the cloud platforms (GCP) to reviewing the algorithms and scientific papers, and publishing papers. I've worked as a research data scientist and machien learning engineer in multidisciplinary teams at the Charles Perkins Centre, the School of Biomedical Engineering and the University of Sydney. I develop technical engineering solutions with focus on investigation, analysis, reporting, and engineering design project. My PhD was on multi-modal signal processing and design and implementation of machine learning algorithsma for automated diagnosis of cardio-respiratory disorders. I worked on development of an innovative automated monitoring system to detect sleep disordered breathing as well as arrhythmia detection. During my postdoctoral research, I collaborated with industry and developed machine learning models for automated diagnosis of sleep apnoea at the presence of cardiovascular disorders. I also worked on personalised medicine and predicting and discovering the links between sleep apnoea and developing cardiovascular disorders in the future. My projects were focused on machine learning, signal processing, statistical analysis and pattern recognition to quantify, identify and predict health conditions such as sleep respiratory disorders and arrhythmias. During my previous research projects, I developed models of acoustic field in treatment by High Intensity Focused Ultrasound (HIFU) as my Master’s degree research thesis. I also designed and developed a prototype of spirometer using differential pressure sensors as my honour’s degree final project. I have experience in lecturing and presentations for several years and provided research supervision to research projects of Engineering students.
...

highlights:

### [Resmed
...

#### Machine Learning Platform Engineer (Current)
...
In my current role, I develop and deploy machine learning solutions that advance healthcare technology while navigating strict regulatory requirements. I recently built an NLP classification model for PHI detection using feature engineering and logistic regression, which automated our data labeling pipeline and reduced manual classification effort by 80% while maintaining HIPAA compliance. I've also led technical evaluation of text-to-SQL conversational AI systems using fine-tuned Llama and GPT-4.1 models, assessing performance, latency, and infrastructure scalability for enterprise healthcare data querying. My work involves architecting ML data pipelines and feature engineering workflows that integrate seamlessly with existing healthcare systems, supporting both model training and inference while preserving data privacy and regulatory compliance. My role uniquely positions me at the intersection of traditional data engineering and emerging AI technologies, where I prototype machine learning platforms, troubleshoot distributed systems, and implement responsible AI solutions within healthcare's privacy constraints.
...
As a graduate student, I learned the fundamentals of data science that prop up Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence. As part of the hands-on learning curriculum, I was frequently tasked with case studies that required my peers and me to apply the best model we had learned to build, whether that be supervised, unsupervised, XBGoost, tree or a variant of a neural net model.  In the program, I focused on language processing models. I fine-tuned many models with different focuses on classification, summarization, generation, and question answering. All of this was before ChatGPT had become public. Thanks to my academic background, this made the popularity of ChatGPT that much more impressive when considering the infrastructure and learning required on the backend of that model.

results.title: Aishwarya C.
highlights:
Product Manager - Global Data & AI Platforms at [Resmed](https://www.linkedin.com/company/resmed)
...
Hey there 👋 I’m a full-stack Data Product Manager who owns the end-to-end data product lifecycle—from designing scalable ingestion pipelines to enabling go-to-market success and downstream adoption. My work lives at the intersection of product strategy, data architecture, and real-world business impact. I lead cross-functional data product initiatives across Product, Marketing, Revenue, IoT, and Legal—each with its own language, needs, and pace. Whether it’s launching privacy-compliant data platforms, driving diagnostic insights, or powering AI/ML use cases, I build products that are technically sound, user-centered, and business-aligned. At ResMed, I drive global initiatives to unify fragmented data systems into trusted, accessible platforms that support therapy onboarding, patient engagement, and strategic decision-making. I work side-by-side with engineering—crafting detailed, forward-thinking requirements, clearing blockers early, and ensuring fast, aligned execution. What sets me apart? I don’t just ship features—I connect technical depth with product intuition and own the go-to-market motion. From discovery and roadmap to delivery and adoption, I make sure the data products we build are used, loved, and driving measurable impact. Whether it’s enabling personalized campaign orchestration for marketing, building GDPR-compliant data infrastructure, or stitching diagnostic and commercial data to power portfolio-level reporting—I lead with clarity, velocity, and ownership. If you’re solving hard problems at the intersection of data, product, and scaled execution or if you’d rather go on a run and talk product under SoCal sunshine—let’s connect. I occasionally share reflections on product, data platforms, and healthcare innovation here: aishmuse.medium.com
...

Govinda has a Master's degree in data science and engineering (M-TECH DSE) as well as a Master's degree in computer applications (MCA). He has more than 16 years of IT experience, including ten years working with big data technologies. He is an expert in Snowflake, Big Data, Traditional Data Warehousing, and Business Intelligence technologies and is a qualified AWS Cloud Data Engineer. The Hadoop Ecosystem, Spark development, and Govinda's 6+ years of practical experience using AWS Cloud services, particularly in ETL pipelines and data migration, are all areas of competence. He stands out due to his excellent communication skills and commitment to staying current in the IT industry. In Govinda's professional career, he has worked for major multinational software services firms including L&T-Infotech, HP Global Services, and Infosys as a Lead Technical Consultant. He offers services to a variety of clients in Snowflake, DBT, Big Data (Cloudera, MapR, Hortonworks), and Business Intelligence - Data Warehousing. He is certified in AWS and IBM Cognos8 BI. In addition to his professional activities, Govinda is a dedicated researcher who focuses on analyzing data insights in the field of health informatics. ...
Exciting times ahead as we continue to innovate and make a meaningful impact for patients around the world. We’re proud to share that Resmed has received U.S. FDA clearance for Personalized Therapy Comfort Settings (PTCS), which will be marketed as Smart Comfort —an AI-enabled medical device that personalizes CPAP therapy comfort settings for people with obstructive sleep apnea (OSA). 

Powered by machine learning and more than 100 million nights of real-world sleep data, Smart Comfort recommends individualized comfort settings to help people with OSA start and stay on CPAP therapy.  

What this means for people with OSA: 
-A smoother, easier start for new users 
-Select comfort settings tailored to each individual
-Supports users in feeling more confident and engaged as they get started with therapy 

For healthcare providers, this means: 
-Easier setup and follow up
-Insight that can help reduce the need for manual adjustments
-More time to focus on clinical decision-making and patient care
...
Smart Comfort will launch in a limited U.S. beta version in early 2026 for new myAir™ users with an AirSense™ 11 device, with a broader U.S. rollout planned later in the year. 

This milestone reflects our ongoing commitment to using technology and real-world evidence to help people sleep better, breathe better and live healthier lives.

results.title: Sr. Research Scientist, AI Experience (Data Scientist 3) | Wfh
highlights:
Sr. Research Scientist, AI Experience (Data Scientist 3) | Wfh
...
# Sr. Research Scientist, AI Experience (Data Scientist 3)
...
As a Research Scientist, you will be a key contributor to Dexcom’s R&D Engineering Team . ​You will be working with the latest cloud computing platforms and AI technologies to establish an ambitious research program supporting our mission, positioning Dexcom as an industry leader in health AI applications. As a thought leader in your field, you will work closely with other R&D stakeholders to design novel model architectures, develop evaluation pipelines, build concept prototypes, and present your work to peers inside and outside the organization.
...
- You help identify long-term research goals as well as intermediate milestones for AI models and applications.
...
- You execute research that pushes forward the state of the art in AI domains such as predictive health outcomes, large language models (LLMs), and Generative AI.
...
- You directly contribute to experiments training AI models, including experimental design, writing reusable code, running evaluations, and preparing results for broader circulation.
...
- You implement new models, tools, and processes used by Dexcom’s core data products team, supporting commercial data product development.
...
- Your track record brainstorming new ideas or improving upon existing ideas in machine learning and AI – including designing and executing experiments – demonstrated by accomplishments such as first author publications or projects.
...
- You are an adept independent researcher, results-driven, and motivated to solve hard technical challenges including choosing impactful research problems and autonomously carrying out long-running projects.
...
- Your experience in creating high-performance implementations of deep learning algorithms, including implementing machine learning and statistical inference algorithms in Pytorch, Tensorflow, etc.
...
- You love being on the cutting edge of machine learning and AI research and have a passion for Dexcom’s mission and health sensing applications.
...
- You have excellent written and verbal communications skills, and an aptitude for leveraging data visualization software and techniques to tell compelling stories using data.
...
- Experience and Education Requirements
...
- Typically requires a Bachelor’s degree in a technical discipline, and a minmum of 5-8 years related experience or Master’s degree and 2-5 years equivalent industry experience or a PhD and 0-2 years experience

results.title: Optum Hiring Data Scientist | Jobformore
highlights:
Optum Hiring Data Scientist | Jobformore
...
# Data Scientist
...
Optum is seeking a Data Science professional who is passionate about applying machine learning, Generative AI, and Large Language Models (LLMs) to solve complex healthcare problems on a scale. Imagine working with massive volumes of healthcare data spanning clinical, claims, behavioral, and digital interactions – powering intelligent personalization, discovery, and decision support for millions of members, providers, and care teams.
...
At Optum, the Data Science mission is to transform healthcare data into robust, scalable AI solutions, including LLM based and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) systems that combine enterprise knowledge with advanced language models to deliver accurate, contextual, and trustworthy insights. These solutions help uncover deep usage patterns, member journeys, and care signals that inform and continuously evolve Optum’s digital products, clinical platforms, and healthcare services.
...
Your work will directly influence how healthcare consumers engage with digital experiences, how clinicians access timely insights, and how business and care strategies are shaped through data. By joining Optum’s data science team, you will be part of an exciting journey to build advanced ML and GenAI driven products, grounded in responsible AI principles, that operate at enterprise scale and create meaningful impact across the healthcare ecosystem.
...
- Design, develop, and deploy advanced analytics, machine learning, and Generative AI solutions to address complex healthcare and business problems using largescale structured and unstructured data
...
- Applies advanced techniques including statistics, machine learning, simulation, and mathematical modelling
...
- Build and optimize LLM based applications, including Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) frameworks, to enable accurate, context aware, and explainable insights from enterprise healthcare data sources such as clinical notes, claims, provider data, and policy documents
...
- Develop, evaluate, and finetune machine learning and GenAI models, including prompt engineering, retrieval strategies, and model evaluation techniques to improve accuracy, relevance, and performance
...
- Translate complex analytical and GenAI outputs into clear, actionable insights for business stakeholders, product teams, and
...
to support data driven decision making
...
- Support prototyping, validation, and deployment of GenAI and ML solutions, working with engineering teams to ensure solutions are scalable, secure, and maintainable. Continuously stay current with advancements in LLMs, GenAI frameworks, and healthcare AI trends, contributing innovative ideas and improvements to Optum’s capabilities
...

...
- Bachelor’s Degree (preferably in information technology, engineering, math, computer science, analytics, engineering or other related field)
...
- 4+ years of experience in handling/active development in data science projects
...
- 4+ years of experience in Microsoft Azure Cloud, Databricks, Mlflow, Spark SQL & Python
...
- Experience using Machine Learning Algorithms: Linear & Logistic Regression, Time Series analysis, Support Vector Machine, K-Means clustering, K-Nearest Neighbors Decision Trees, Random Forest, Naive Bayes, PCA, SVD, Artificial Neural Networks, Association Rules, Genetic Algorithm, Optimization, Bagging and Boosting algorithms etc.
...
- Understanding of data, schema, data model, machine learning and how to bring efficiency in big data related life cycle
...

highlights:
Principal Data Scientist - Generative AI, Machine Learning, Python, R - Remote | Wfh
...
# Principal Data Scientist - Generative AI, Machine Learning, Python, R - Remote
...
Responsible for overseeing data science projects, managing and mentoring a team, and aligning data initiatives with business goals. Lead the development and implementation of data models, collaborate with cross-functional teams, and stay updated on industry trends. Ensure ethical data use and communicate complex technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders. Lead initiatives on model governance and model ops to align with regulatory and security requirements. This role requires technical expertise, strategic thinking, and leadership to drive data-driven decision-making within the organization and be the pioneer on generative AI healthcare solutions, aimed at revolutionizing healthcare operations as well as enhancing member experience.
...
- Research and Development: Stay current with the latest advancements in AI and machine learning and apply these insights to improve existing models and develop new methodologies.
...
- AI Model Deployment, Monitoring & Model Governance: Deploy AI models into production environments, monitor their performance, and adjust as necessary to maintain accuracy and effectiveness and meet all governance and regulatory requirements.
...
- Innovation Projects: Lead pilot projects to test and implement new AI technologies within the organization
...
- Machine Learning Model Development: Design, develop, and train machine learning models using a variety of algorithms and techniques, including supervised and unsupervised learning, deep learning, and reinforcement learning.
...
- Agentic Workflows Implementation: Develop and implement agentic workflows that utilize AI agents for autonomous task execution, enhancing operational efficiency and decision-making capabilities.
...
- RAG Pattern Utilization: Employ retrieval-augmented generation patterns to improve the performance of language models, ensuring they can access and utilize external knowledge effectively to enhance their outputs.
...
- Model Fine-Tuning: Fine-tune pre-trained models to adapt them to specific tasks or datasets, ensuring optimal performance and relevance in various applications.
...
- Collaboration: Work closely with cross-functional teams, including software engineers, product managers, and business analysts, to integrate AI solutions into existing systems and processes.
...
- Partner closely with business and other technology teams to build ML models which helps in improving Star ratings, reduce care gap and other business objectives.
...
Master's Degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Statistics, or a related field
...
- 10+ years' work experience as a data scientist preferably in healthcare environment but candidates with suitable experience in other industries will be considered
...
- Technical Proficiency: Strong programming skills in languages such as Python and R, and experience with machine learning frameworks like TensorFlow, Keras, or PyTorch.
...
- Statistical Analysis: Excellent understanding of statistical methods and machine learning algorithms, including k-NN, Naive Bayes, SVM, and neural networks.
...
- Experience with Agentic Workflows: Familiarity with designing and implementing agentic workflows that leverage AI agents for autonomous operations.
...
- RAG Techniques: Knowledge of retrieval-augmented generation techniques and their application in enhancing AI model outputs.
...
- Model Fine-Tuning Expertise: Proven experience in fine-tuning models for specific tasks, ensuring they meet the required performance metrics.
...
PREFERRED EDUCATION:
...
PHD or additional experience
...
- PREFERRED EXPERIENCE:
- Experience with cloud platforms (e.g., Databricks, Snowflake, Azure AI Studio etc.) for working with AI workflows and deploying models.
- Familiarity with natural language processing (NLP) and computer vision techniques.

results.title: LLM Engineer job with VIB | 12860950
highlights:
LLM Engineer job with VIB | 12860950
...
# LLM Engineer
...
VIB is a leading life sciences research institute in Flanders, Belgium, with a strong focus on translating basic scientific discoveries into benefits for society. With the launch of VIB.AI, we are expanding our mission to harness the power of artificial intelligence for life sciences research, innovation, and impact.
...
The VIB Center for Neuroscience Leuven (CNL) investigates the mechanisms of brain function and neurological disease across the scales of biology, spanning neurodegeneration, neurodevelopment, synaptic biology, and computational neuroscience. AI and large language models have transformative potential across these research areas.
...
We are now looking for an LLM Engineer in a shared position between VIB.AI's Machine Learning Expertise Unit and the Center for Neuroscience. You will split your time between building institute-wide AI tools and applying them directly to neuroscience research challenges.
...
As an LLM Engineer in this shared position, you will:
...
- Develop and deploy LLM-based pilot projects tailored to neuroscience research at the CNL, applying AI to questions in molecular, cellular and circuit neuroscience, neurodegeneration, neurodevelopment and disease mechanisms.
...
- Build and maintain components of VIB's institutional intelligence layer, including agentic bioinformatics workflows, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems, and AI-assisted experimental design tools.
...
- Design, implement, and evaluate LLM-based solutions for scientific text mining, multimodal integration of literature and biological data, and knowledge extraction to support discovery.
...
- Collaborate with wet-lab and computational neuroscience researchers to identify opportunities where generative AI and LLMs can accelerate scientific progress.
...
- Work with the Machine Learning Expertise Unit and VIB's AI Studio to translate pilot projects into practical tools that serve the broader VIB community.
...
- Promote ethical, transparent, and responsible use of LLMs in line with best practices and international standards.
...
- Mine opportunities and data at the center’s advanced technology expertise centers and affiliated central core facilities.
...
- Leverage VIB's Data Core services, including high-performance computing pipelines and large-scale GPU resources, to scale LLM development and deployment.
...
- PhD in machine learning, computer science, computational biology, computational neuroscience, statistics, or a related field.
- Strong experience with large language models, including pretraining, fine-tuning, prompt engineering, and evaluation.
- Knowledge of modern deep learning frameworks (e.g., PyTorch, JAX, TensorFlow).
- Experience with real-world applications of LLMs in scientific or biomedical contexts is a plus.
...
- Familiarity with neuroscience data types (imaging, electrophysiology, genomics, proteomics, or clinical data) is a plus but not required.
...
- Ability to work collaboratively across disciplines and communicate effectively with researchers from different backgrounds.
- A strong interest in applying LLMs to impactful life science and neuroscience problems.
...
- The opportunity to pioneer applications of LLMs in neuroscience and the broader life sciences.
...
- A unique position at the interface of an AI platform team and a world-class neuroscience research center, with exposure to both engineering and scientific challenges.
...
- Direct access to VIB's Data Core services, which provide expert support in data management and high-performance computing, including optimized pipelines and large-scale GPU resources.
...
Please complete the application through the VIB online application tool. A complete application file should contain the following documents:
...
For further information, please send an email to stein.aerts@kuleuven.be or joost.schymkowitz@kuleuven.be with subject title Inquiry: LLM Engineer CNL/VIB.AI.

results.title: Senior Manager, Artificial Intelligence Data Engineer job with Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, Inc. | 3058625
highlights:
Senior Manager, Artificial Intelligence Data Engineer job with Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, Inc. | 3058625
...
# Senior Manager, Artificial Intelligence Data Engineer
...
Employer Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, Inc. Location Tarrytown, New York Start date Jul 2, 2026
...
Global Development is embarking on a Digital Transformation project incorporating AI, machine learning, and automation to help us reduce cycle times, improve quality allowing us to focus on more meaningful work. We focus on developing and improving data pipelines, infrastructure, architecture, and analytic tools to allow resources to fuel our transformation. Working with a team of engineers, analysts, and scientists, you will contribute to modernizing our clinical data infrastructure. This highly visible role will be a technical and strategic liaison between the transformation projects and IT.
...
A key responsibility will be developing and optimizing schema and data models for clinical data. We ensure that those products are standard compliant and interoperable with other platforms and data sources. This role will also contribute to data governance strategy and to the development and management of tools improving and monitoring data quality. The data engineer will also develop processes to automate routine workflows. The engineer will also provide technical leadership and mentorship and will stay current with innovations in data engineering so that they may be evaluated for implementation. This position offers the opportunity to contribute to a fast-growing, science-driven organization making a meaningful difference to patients worldwide.
...
Discover your role: Work with a cross-functional team to optimize and implement our data strategy with a focus on optimization for digital transformation and the use of AI/ML Design and document end-to-end data architectures that support diverse analytic, operational, and research needs. Facilitate the implementation of a modern data platform (e.g. Snowflake, Databricks, etc.) Identify opportunities and implement solutions to increase data interoperability and standardization among systems and across other business units. Develop and implement pipelines to monitor and improve both internal and external (i.e., from CRO partners) data quality. Work with informatics and AI engineers optimizing the utility of data for their respective pipelines. Monitor and optimize the performance of data architectures and platforms. Develop or implement critical metrics to measure the impact of the overall data strategy. Stay up to date with the latest advances in the field and, as appropriate, evaluate them for adoption
...
This role requires:An advanced degree in computer science, statistics, biomedical informatics, or a related field is preferred (PhD + 2 years of experience or an MS + 4 years of relevant experience). A minimum of 5 years experience developing and leading the implementation of data engineering solutions, including accountability for the success of the implementation, in life sciences or healthcare Demonstrated expertise in designing and maintaining infrastructure and architecture for clinical or biomedical data in a healthcare or life sciences setting. Expertise in modern data platforms (e.g., Snowflake, Redshift, BigQuery, Databricks) and programming languages such as Python, SQL, R, etc. Maintain and manage code repositories (e.g Bitbucket) ensuring clean, well-documented code with proper version control. Proficiency in cloud architecture (e.g. AWS, Azure, GCP) and DevOps practices. Recognized certifications are a plus. Experience building, scaling, and maintaining pipelines for structured and unstructured data. Ability to integrate pipelines across the enterprise is essential. Deep understanding of regulatory frameworks (HIPAA, GDPR, 21 CFR Part 11) and clinical data standards (CDISC, HL7, FHIR). Knowledge of machine learning pipelines and integration with clinical data platforms. May require travel up to 20%.

results.title: Principal Machine Learning Engineer- LLM Fine-tuning and Optimization | CareerSync
highlights:
Principal Machine Learning Engineer- LLM Fine-tuning and Optimization | CareerSync
...
# Principal Machine Learning Engineer- LLM Fine-tuning and Optimization
...
Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence are at the heart of the Airbnb product. From Trust to Payments, and from Customer Service to Marketing we rely on ML to ensure that guests and hosts have the best possible experience with Airbnb.
...
The CS AI product team is responsible for driving CSxAI (Customer Support x Artificial Intelligence) initiatives by adopting the Generative AI technologies to enable an intelligent, scalable and exceptional service experience. The team develops and enhances various AI models, ML services and tools including LLM fine-tuning, alignment and optimization, RAG/Search, LLM evaluation and testing automation, feedback-based learning and guardrail for a wide range of applications in Airbnb.
...
As a principal machine learning engineer, you will be responsible for fine-tuning state-of-the-art LLMs for diverse use cases while optimizing models for high-performance deployment on Airbnb’s ML Infrastructure. You will partner with product managers, software engineers, data scientists and operation teams to brainstorm, design and develop AI products such as AI Assistant, Autonomous agent, recommendation, travel planning, and many more products that make meaningful impacts in the world of travel.
...
- Work with large scale structured and unstructured data; explore, experiment, build and continuously improve foundation models for Airbnb product, business and operational use cases.
...
- Create a multi-year tech roadmap that enables our team to stay on the leading edge of the rapidly evolving AI landscape and leverage the best in class technologies to deliver customer benefits.
...
- Continuously evaluate recent and upcoming large foundational models, ensuring the selection and refinement of the highest quality models for enhanced performance and efficiency.
...
- Hands-on prototype, develop and productionize LLM models and pipelines at scale, including both batch and real-time use cases.
...
- Drive key AI architectural decisions for products, and contribute to Airbnb’s ML platform architecture and strategy.
...
- Minimum Qualifications:
...
- PhD in Computer Science, Machine Learning, Mathematics, Statistics, or related technical field.
- 10+ years of experience with developing machine learning models and products at scale from inception to business impact.
...
- Programming experience in Python and hands-on experience with frameworks such as PyTorch.
...
- Proven record of training, fine tuning, optimizing models and inference run-time
...
- Post-training experience in areas like data processing for fine-tuning; responsible LLMs; LLM alignment; reinforcement learning; efficient training and inference; language model evaluation; and/or multilingual and multimodal modeling.
...
- Or specialized experience in runtime optimizations, model quantization, compression, on-device inference, GPU inference, pytorch, kernel development
...
- Preferred Qualifications:
...
- PhD in AI, machine learning, data science, or related technical fields.

- Publications at peer-reviewed AI conferences (e.g., NeurIPS, CVPR, ICML, ICLR, ICCV, and ACL).
...
- Customer Support Systems: Experience with AI technologies in customer support applications.
...
- Agile Practice for AI production: Experience with the entire AI product development lifecycle from incubation to production at scale, following agile practices in the Applied AI/ML domain.
...
- Infrastructure Acumen: Experience deploying and scaling business-critical AI services and driving architectural requirements on ML infrastructures

results.title: Job Board
highlights:
# Research Fellow / Research Scientist Generative AI, Vision-Language Models, and Medical Image Analysis
...
Contact Email for the Job Positing: sila.kurugol@childrens.harvard.edu Organization: Harvard Medical School and Boston Children's HospitalLocation: Boston, USATitle: Research Fellow / Research Scientist Generative AI, Vision-Language Models, and Medical Image AnalysisURL: https://labs.childrenshospital.org/files/quin/files/documents/quin_ai_job_ad_bch_hms.pdfClosing date: October 1, 2026 Description:
...
Research Fellow / Research Scientist @Harvard: Generative AI, Vision-Language Models, and Medical Image Analysis
...
- Develop AI pipelines for clinical text, radiology reports, electronic health record data, and medical images.
- Work with LLMs, vision-language models, multimodal models, and generative AI tools for healthcare applications.
...
- Run, adapt, and improve existing code for clinical NLP, LLM-based information extraction, and medical image analysis.
...
- Develop algorithms for segmentation, object detection, weak labeling, annotation-efficient learning, and multimodal disease characterization.
...
- Validate AI outputs against expert clinician annotations and clinically relevant reference standards.
...
- Technically strong, highly motivated, and genuinely interested in learning. Good track record of publications.
- Able to take ownership of active projects and move them toward publication, grant deliverables, and clinical translation.
- Well suited for a recent Master’s graduate, PhD graduate, or postdoctoral researcher with strong AI/ML skills.
...
- Master’s degree, PhD, or equivalent experience in computer science, biomedical engineering, electrical engineering, data science, medical informatics, computational imaging, or a related field.
- Strong programming skills in Python.
- Experience with machine learning or deep learning frameworks such as PyTorch, TensorFlow, Hugging Face, MONAI, or similar tools.
- Strong interest in generative AI, vision-language models, LLMs, medical image analysis, and clinical AI.
- Ability to work independently and collaborate closely with clinicians and technical team members.
...
- LLMs, vision-language models, prompt engineering, retrieval-augmented generation, clinical NLP, or structured information extraction.
- Medical image analysis, including segmentation, object detection, registration, image classification, weak supervision, or foundation models.
...
The candidate will join a collaborative research environment at Boston Children’s Hospital and Harvard Medical School, working at the interface of artificial intelligence, medical imaging, clinical LLMs, multimodal learning, and pediatric medicine. Interested candidates should send a CV, a brief statement of research interests, and examples of relevant code, publications, or projects to: sila.kurugol@childrens.harvard.edu
